meaning: adj.闉瓙浼肩殑,鍍忛灜瀛愮殑
examples: They have a whiplike structure called a flagellum.
BIOLOGY: Certain bacteria propel themselves using a flagellum, a whiplike tail.
Certain eukaryotic cells can swim freely in liquid environments, propelled by whiplike cilia or flagella.
Whiplike cords of mint raced across the flagstones.
